The Importance of Sport for The Children’s Health
Sport helps children to develop both physically and mentally. Physical activity should be part of our daily life.
Practicing any kind of sport from an early age helps children in many ways, for example, group activities improve
interaction and social integration. It also improves the mental level and promotes a healthy lifestyle in adulthood,
as the habits we acquire as children are often to stay. Sports have multiple healthy benefits. If we talk on a
psychological or personality level, sports activities (especially those done in groups) help children to strengthen
their social self-esteem, and allow them to understand the importance of having and respecting the rules.
Another aspect to consider is what type of sport or activity will suit best the child considering his/her conditions
and abilities. A more timid and shy child is likely to get better at a sport where she/he has to relate to peers. To a
more active one, an individual sport such as tennis can help better develop her/his ability to concentrate. One of
the perhaps most relevant issues is the importance of physical exercise, not only in children but also in young and
old people, because moderate daily physical exercise helps prevent overweight and obesity. Taking into account
the growth of the percentage of children and young people with obesity and overweight in the Western world, the
inclusion of sport in the family routine becomes almost mandatory.
Sport can also have the power to help us rest. For all those fathers and mothers of active children, try to get them
to run every day playing soccer, or swimming or any other activity that keeps them physically active. They will fall
asleep in bed in a matter of seconds and you, dads and moms, too.

MINISTÈRE DE L’ÉDUCATION NATIONALE ET DE LA FORMATION PROFESSIONNELLE (MENFP)
FILIÈRE D’ENSEIGNEMENT GÉNÉRAL
EXAMENS DE FIN D’ETUDES SECONDAIRES
ANGLAIS
SÉRIES : (SVT, SES, SMP)
JUILLET 2022
Consignes : 1. L’usage de la calculatrice programmable est formellement interdit.
2. Tout gadget électronique (Tél., tablette, iPad, montre intelligente) est formellement interdit dans la salle d’examen.
3. Le silence est obligatoire dans la salle, il crée de meilleures conditions de travail.
Durée de l’épreuve : 2 heures
.
Climate change - Can we stop it?
Twenty years ago, people could perhaps imagine that climate change was not a real problem, because
some scientists still had doubts. Today almost all the world„s scientists agree: the world is getting hotter,
and it is the fault of human beings. Our planet is going towards a climate catastrophe, but we can stop its
worst effects. Indeed we must do everything possible to stop them.
In November 2021, world leaders (or most of them) met in Glasgow, in Scotland, for the COP26 summit.


They made quite a few decisions, to try and stop the Earth getting too warm. Many countries promised to become “carbon neutral” before 2050. Some have promised to become “carbon neutral” even faster. World leaders agree that we have to stop using coal and oil to generate heat and electricity; we should use green energy sources instead known as “renewables”. Before the year 2100, all the energy that we use may perhaps come from renewable sources: the sun, the sea, the wind and rivers can produce more than enough energy for everyone on our planet, but we still have a long way to go. The green energy revolution has already begun, but the challenges are still enormous. “Going green” will cost an enormous amount of money, and use some kinds of technology that do not yet exist. Also there are some people who believe that it will be too expensive. Although most people care about our planet and know that we have to act now to avoid a climate catastrophe, there are other people who have different priorities. There are people who just don’t care,

and there are people who care more about themselves than about the world around them. There are
people and big companies that just want to make money, and are not interested in anything that gets in
their way. Most importantly, there are politicians who are frightened of doing anything that other people will
not like; they make promises, but they do not keep them.
Northstar, Longmansecond. Edition (Carolyne D. Sandini)1997.
I. Compétence Interprétative (30 pts.)
A- Read the text carefully answer the following questions. (20 pts.)
1- Where did world leaders meet in November 2021?
2- Name two renewable sources mentioned in the text.
3- Why aren‟t some companies interested in caring about our planet?
4- What has been promised by the year 2050?
5- According to the author, who always makes promises and never keeps them?
B- Summarize the text in five complete sentences (10 pts.)
II. Compétence Linguistique (30 pts.)
A. Choose the best answer among the possibilities below (10 pts.)
1. Thomas ________________________his vacation for several months.


a) planning b) has been planning c) is planning d) been planning for.
2. Roberto wasn‟t in class today, ____________________?
a) was he b) did he c) wasn‟t he d) has he
3. You didn‟t damage my car, _____________?
a) did you b) has you c) didn‟t you d) were you
4. Edmond enjoys hiking and __________________.
a) to camp b) fishing c) to fish d) to climb mountain
5. The new system is more productive and_____________________.
a) cheap b) cheaper c) the cheapest d) more cheap

B- . Turn the following sentences into active or passive voice (10 pts.)
1- I made this pie with onions and cheese.
2- Everyone considers her to be the best student in the class.
3- I was told the joke by my sister.
4- This room was decorated by my mother.
5- They will have built the new bridge by the end of the year.
C- Select the right adjective that best suits in each sentence (10 pts.)
1. I was (disappointing / disappointed) by the movie. I had expected it to be more interesting.
2. Are you (interesting / interested) in soccer?


3. It‟s sometimes (embarrassing / embarrassed) when you have to ask people for money.
4. I had never expected to get the job. I was really (surprising / surprised) when it was offered to me.
5. We were all (fascinating/fascinated) by the play.
I II. Compétence pragmatique et interpersonnelle (20 pts.)
Problem solving-situation
A classmate of the opposite sex has the habit of leaning over your desk a bit too close to you for
comfort. Sometimes, the classmate will place an arm around your shoulder or purposely touch you. You
do not want to embarrass or anger your classmate. How would you react?
Write a short paragraph to talk about your reaction.
IV. Compétence discursive et argumentative (20 pts.)
Choose one of the following topics and write a twelve-line paragraph about it.
1. As far as you are concerned as a S4 student, what actions can you personally suggest to take in order to
contribute to the reduction of the climate change effects? State your insight in a chronological pattern.


2. Why is it important for people to protect their environment? State your reasons with clear and supporting
details.
END OF EXAMINATION …

World leaders agree that we have to stop using coal and oil to generate heat and electricity; we should use green energy sources instead known as “renewables”. Before the year 2100, all the energy that we use may perhaps come from renewable sources: the sun, the sea, the wind and rivers can produce more than enough energy for everyone on our planet, but we still have a long way to go. The green energy revolution has already begun, but the challenges are still enormous. “Going green” will cost an enormous amount of money, and use some kinds of technology that do not yet exist. Also there are some people who believe that it will be too expensive. Although most people care about our planet and know that we have to act now to avoid a climate catastrophe, there are other people who have different priorities. There are people who just don’t care, and there are people who care more about themselves than about the world around them. There are people and big companies that just want to make money, and are not interested in anything that gets in their way. Most importantly, there are politicians who are frightened of doing anything that other people will not like; they make promises, but they do not keep them.
Northstar, Longmansecond. Edition (Carolyne D. Sandini)1997.

Durée de l’épreuve : 2 heures  

Organic Food  
Organic food is very popular. It is also expensive. Some organic food costs twice as much as non-organic food.  
New parents and pet owners pay up to 200% more for organic food. Some people think organic food is a waste of money. There is one main difference between organic and non-organic food. Organic farms do not use agricultural chemicals, such as pesticides. In many countries organic foods have special labels. These guarantee that the products are natural.

Some people think organic means locally grown. Originally this was true. Over time organic farming became more difficult. The demand for organic food grew larger than the supply. Small companies had to sell out to large companies. There weren’t enough organic ingredients, such as grain and cattle. This made it difficult for many organic companies to stay in business. Today, many large companies have an organic line of products. Is organic food more nutritious? This is part of the debate. Many farmers and consumers believe it is. They think agricultural chemicals cause health problems such as cancer or allergies. Many health professionals disagree. Few studies prove that organic foods prevent health problems. Health specialists worry more about bacteria, such as salmonella. These can come into contact with organic and non-organic food. Doctors recommend washing produce very carefully. Handling meat carefully is important too. Most people agree that naturally grown food tastes better. Is tastier food worth the extra money? This is a matter

of opinion. Whether it is healthier or not may require more research. However, organic consumers argue it is
better to be safe than sorry.
Reader’s Digest 1999
I. Compétence Interprétative (30 pts.)
A. Read the text carefully then answer the following questions (20 pts.)
1- What are the reasons why young people turn to alcohol?
2- How do some observers qualify today’s youth?
3- What particular problem do young women face?
4- Are all the young people in trouble?
5- Why are so many youngsters committing crime?
B- Summarize the text in (5) five sentences (10 pts.)
II. Compétence Linguistique (30 pts.)
A. Write the correct form of the verb in parentheses (10 pts.)
1) Is it important that we____________ there? (be)
2) I suggest that you don’t take the job without______________ the salary. (renegotiate)
3) I won’t know the answer, but I wish I___________________ it. (know)
4) An insomniac_______________ at night. (sleep, not)
5) How can parents get their children____________ more? (read)
B. Change to passive or active voice (10 pts.)
1. Do they teach History at this school?


2. I saw them preparing their bags, didn’t I?
3. You weren’t bitten by the dog.
4. The bill can’t be paid by Sue.
5. They were carrying the injured players off the field.

C. Select the right word to complete the sentences (10 pts.)
crutches – editorial – castle – amnesia - flood
1. A condition that causes people to lose their memory: ________________________
2. A solid structure that was usually fortified against an attack: ___________________
3. A large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry: ____________________
4. She has broken her leg and needs _____________________
5. An article in a newspaper or magazine that expresses the opinion of the editor or publisher: _________________

Problem-solving situation
Mario is at the wedding reception of a co-worker. His boss and several other co-workers are guests as well. When
the reception ends, Mario knows his boss has been drinking all evening. The boss offers to give Mario and his co-


workers, a ride home. Mario does not think his boss should drive. What can Mario do? How can he kindly deal with
this issue?
Write a short paragraph to answer the questions above.
I V . Compétence discursive et argumentative (20 pts)
Choose one of the two given topics and write a twelve- line paragraph about it
1- What reasons do you personally have for choosing organic or non- organic food?
2- What should you do to avoid going to the doctor very often? Come up with key details.
END OF EXAMINATION …

MINISTÈRE DE L’ÉDUCATION NATIONALE ET DE LA FORMATION PROFESSIONNELLE (MENFP)
FILIÈRE D’ENSEIGNEMENT GÉNÉRAL
EXAMENS DE FIN D’ETUDES SECONDAIRES
ANGLAIS
SÉRIES : (SVT, SES, SMP)
JUILLET 2022
Consignes : 1. L’usage de la calculatrice programmable est formellement interdit.
2. Tout gadget électronique (Tél., tablette, iPad, montre intelligente) est formellement interdit dans la salle d’examen.
3. Le silence est obligatoire dans la salle, il crée de meilleures conditions de travail.
Durée de l’épreuve : 2 heures
.
Natural ressources
People who live in or near the Tai forest know the value of their trees and use them for many purposes. They
also know that some logging companies break the rules which control their work and cut down more trees
than they are permitted to. Very often they also cut down those which are useful to the local inhabitants. As a
result, the people complain and try to make the companies obey the regulations.
Tai’s plants have been used as medicines by traditional doctors for a long time because they contain certain


substances. Some of these substances, such as the alkaloids found in a plant called henbane, are used for
the relief of pain and insomnia. Other substances are used to treat rheumatism, Salicin, for example, which
occurs in the African willow tree, is one of these.
Like the inhabitants of Tai, scientists also realize the value of its plants and are carrying out research. They
identify the chemical substances in the plants and study these substances to see if they have medicinal uses.
If they do, Cote d’Ivoire will not need to import so many drugs. The forests plants will provide other benefits,
too. Research will enable scientists to develop varieties of crops which resist diseases.
www.myenglishpages.com
I. Compétence Interprétative (30 pts.)
A. Read the text carefully, and then answer the following questions about it (20 pts.)
1. Name a bad action that the logging companies sometimes do.
2. The text mentioned two types of people who know the importance of the Tai forest plants. Who
are they?
3. According to the text, what type of plant is used to relieve insomnia?


4. Explain how scientists are carrying out research on Tai Forest.
5. According to the text, what is the main use of Salicin?
B. - Summarize the text in four complete sentences (10 pts.)
II . C ompétence linguistique (30 points)
A. Turn into passive voice the following sentences. (10 pts.)
1. They would take the opportunity.
2. My brother gives me three dollars every day.
3. People speak Portuguese in Brazil.
4. My grandfather built two houses in 1943.
5. Did he write three books last year?
B. Match the words of column A to their meaning in column B (10 pts.)
A B
1- Conservation efforts a) the ongoing relationship between living things and their environment.
2- Ecosystem b) plants and animals living in a natural environment
3- Food chain c) measures to protect and restore the natural world
4- Global warming d) the gradual increase in temperature on earth
5- Wildlife e) a group of organisms that feed off of each other
C. Select the right letter to complete the sentences below (10 pts.)
1. Our friends live in this house. It’s our_____________.


a) friends’ house b) house of friends c) friend’s house d) house for friends
2. John behaves as if he ________________ a baby.
a) were b) is c) be d) will be
3. We still have _________work to do.
a) a great deal of b) several c) many d) a few
4. A book of maps that travelers often carry on trips is called___________.
a) an ocean b) an atlas c) a cliff d) a mountain
5. I hardly__________ watch TV.
a) never b) yet c) already d) ever

III. Compétence Pragmatique et interpersonnelle (20 pts.)
Problem-solving situation
Hello, my name is Jenna, and I am a senior in college. I will be graduating this semester. Last week, my
university offered me a full scholarship for MA studies .This scholarship is only offered to students with
excellent grades like me. My parents do not think I should accept this scholarship. They want me to work
in the family business instead. This is a great opportunity that I don’t want to lose. What should I do?
Write a short paragraph to provide me with your advice.
I V . C ompétence discursive et argumentative (20 pts.)


Choose one of the two given topics and write a twelve-line paragraph about it.  
1. Why is it important for people to protect their environment? State your reasons with clear and supporting details.  
2. Some entrepreneurs believed that paying their workers more would get them to work better, and become better consumers. Express your pros and cons about his financial approach?  
END OF EXAMINATION …

Répondez aux questions suivantes concernant le texte ci-dessous :
1. Name a bad action that the logging companies sometimes do.
2. The text mentioned two types of people who know the importance of the Tai forest plants. Who are they?
3. According to the text, what type of plant is used to relieve insomnia?
4. Explain how scientists are carrying out research on Tai Forest.
5. According to the text, what is the main use of Salicin?
items.texte:
People who live in or near the Tai forest know the value of their trees and use them for many purposes. They also know that some logging companies break the rules which control their work and cut down more trees than they are permitted to. Very often they also cut down those which are useful to the local inhabitants. As a result, the people complain and try to make the companies obey the regulations.
Tai’s plants have been used as medicines by traditional doctors for a long time because they contain certain substances. Some of these substances, such as the alkaloids found in a plant called henbane, are used for the relief of pain and insomnia. Other substances are used to treat rheumatism, Salicin, for example, which occurs in the African willow tree, is one of these.
Like the inhabitants of Tai, scientists also realize the value of its plants and are carrying out research. They identify the chemical substances in the plants and study these substances to see if they have medicinal uses. If they do, Cote d’Ivoire will not need to import so many drugs. The forests plants will provide other benefits, too. Research will enable scientists to develop varieties of crops which resist diseases.

items.enonce: Résumez le texte ci-dessus en quatre phrases complètes.

A. Transformez les phrases suivantes en voix passive :
1. They would take the opportunity.
2. My brother gives me three dollars every day.
3. People speak Portuguese in Brazil.
4. My grandfather built two houses in 1943.
5. Did he write three books last year?

B. Associez chaque mot de la colonne A avec sa signification correspondante dans la colonne B.
A | B
1- Conservation efforts | c) measures to protect and restore the natural world
2- Ecosystem | a) the ongoing relationship between living things and their environment.
3- Food chain | e) a group of organisms that feed off of each other
4- Global warming | d) the gradual increase in temperature on earth
5- Wildlife | b) plants and animals living in a natural environment
